title: Handle for a personal care implement and personal care implement
abstract: A handle for a personal care implement includes a connector for repeatedly attaching and detaching a head to and from the handle. The handle has a longitudinal extension extending between a proximal end closest to the head and a distal end opposite to the proximal end, the handle further having a core structure, the core structure and the connector being unitarily made to form a core-connector unit. A second component at least partially covers the core structure, wherein the core structure has a longitudinal extension extending along the longitudinal extension of the handle, the core structure comprising at least two protrusions interlocking with the second component.
claims: 1. A handle ( 12 ) for a personal care implement ( 10 ), the handle ( 12 ) comprising a connector ( 16 ) for repeatedly attaching and detaching a head ( 14 ) to and from the handle ( 12 ) with a substantially linear motion in an axial direction of the handle, the handle ( 12 ) having a longitudinal extension ( 28 ) extending between a proximal end ( 30 ) closest to the head ( 14 ) and a distal end ( 32 ) opposite to the proximal end ( 30 ), the handle ( 12 ) further comprising: a core structure ( 18 ), the core structure ( 18 ) and the connector ( 16 ) being unitarily made to form a core-connector unit ( 20 ), and an overmolded second component ( 22 ) that is at least partially covering the core structure ( 18 ) such that the core structure ( 18 ) is at least partially embedded in the overmolded second component ( 22 ), wherein the overmolded second component ( 22 ) comprises polymeric material, wherein the core structure ( 18 ) has a longitudinal extension ( 34 ) extending at least 75% along the longitudinal extension ( 28 ) of the handle ( 12 ), the core structure ( 18 ) comprising a plurality of protrusions ( 36 ) disposed along the length extension ( 34 ) of the core structure ( 18 ) interlocking with and embedded in the overmolded second component ( 22 ), the plurality of protrusions ( 36 ) extending from the longitudinal extension ( 34 ) of the core structure ( 18 ) in a substantially orthogonal direction, wherein each of the plurality of protrusions ( 36 ) forms a structure selected from the group consisting of a rib, a fin, a bar, a bridge, and a nub.
